roster_integrity.universe_note: ETHA and IBIT (Digital-Asset-ETF sleeve) appear in the data drop but are absent from portfolios.json. portfolios.json covers 50 of 52 priced holdings. universe_incomplete=true until corrected.

summary.headline: Persisting AI-chip rout (Friday June 26 close, pre-market Monday). LRCX and MRVL escalated to RED via thesis cross-check — WFE cyclicality and AI capex moderation risks now materializing. CAT improving: tariff estimate revised down to $2.2-2.4B, pre-market bounce +1.16%. CRDO stabilized. QCOM sell-off continuing. Hyperscaler late-July earnings window is primary forward risk.

flags.headline: CRDO -10.5%: AI capex cycle risk materializing; stabilized — no further cascade
flags.detail: Credo Technology dropped 10.5% on Friday June 26 ($268.13 → $239.91), driven by SK Hynix HBM4 production slowdown (strategic redirect to conventional DRAM) and Broadcom post-earnings capex guidance miss vs. elevated expectations. HBM4 ramp pushed Q2→Q3 2026. CRDO is ~23% below its June 22 all-time high of $308.67. As of Monday pre-market, the sell-off has stabilized — no further cascade. Analyst community remains bullish: BofA raised PT to $340, Stifel to $350. Consensus remains Strong Buy (18 analysts, 0 Sell).
flags.thesis_link: Matches CRDO key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle dependency — a slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending due to ROI concerns or macroeconomic headwinds would directly reduce demand for Credo's products given near-total AI exposure.' The SK Hynix HBM4 slowdown and Broadcom capex guidance miss are direct proxies for this risk materializing.

evidence.source: TechPowerUp — SK Hynix slows HBM4 ramp
ZeroHedge — Korean article that sparked the selloff

flags.headline: QCOM -8.0%: Memory shortage confirmed; sell-off continuing into Monday
flags.detail: Qualcomm fell 8.0% on Friday June 26 ($205.01 → $188.615). As of Monday pre-market, QCOM remains around $189 — no bounce. June is on track for the worst month for QCOM in ~7 years (-18% MTD). The global memory shortage constraining handset production was directly cited by CEO Amon and could persist into FY2027-28. Apple modem displacement risk (est. 20-25% of QCT chip revenue) remains a separate long-term overhang. Analyst consensus mixed: Benchmark Buy $300, RBC Hold $250, BofA Underperform $220.
flags.thesis_link: Matches QCOM key_risk #1: 'Global memory shortage constraining handset production — Q2 FY2026 guidance cut reflects supply limitations that CEO Amon warns could persist into FY2027-28, compressing near-term revenue growth.'

flags.headline: KEYS -7.64%: Strong Q2 results; drop is valuation-driven profit-taking at 51x trailing P/E
flags.detail: Keysight fell 7.64% on Friday June 26 ($360.15 → $332.625) despite reporting 31% AI-segment revenue growth in Q2 FY2026. Both AI capex dependency risk and valuation compression risk materializing simultaneously. GF Value flagged ~66% overvalued. KEYS had run +99.3% YoY, +61.8% YTD. No company-specific negative news found. Thesis intact operationally.
flags.thesis_link: Matches KEYS key_risks: (1) 'AI capex cycle dependency — any slowdown in hyperscaler data center spending would directly impact Keysight's fastest-growing segments'; (2) 'Valuation compression risk — at 51.2x trailing P/E and 28.3x forward P/E, KEYS requires continued 20%+ growth; any deceleration could trigger significant multiple contraction.' Both risks materializing simultaneously.

flags.headline: LRCX -5.93%: SK Hynix HBM4 delay directly reduces near-term WFE demand — escalated RED via thesis cross-check
flags.detail: Lam Research fell 5.93% on Friday June 26 ($401.83 → $377.99), ~7.5% below its June 22 all-time high of $409.54. The SK Hynix HBM4 ramp delay (Q2→Q3) directly reduces near-term Lam tool orders (HBM etch and deposition steps). This matches LRCX key_risk #1 and triggers severity escalation from AMBER to RED. Analyst community bullish: Citi target $450, BofA $480 Buy. LRCX up +114.8% YTD.
flags.thesis_link: Matches LRCX key_risk #1: 'Semiconductor equipment cyclicality — WFE spending has historically experienced 20-30% peak-to-trough swings, and the current AI capex boom could face correction if hyperscaler ROI on AI infrastructure disappoints.' SK Hynix HBM4 ramp delay (Q2→Q3) is a direct near-term reduction in WFE demand. Severity escalated AMBER → RED per thesis-match protocol.

evidence.source: TechPowerUp — SK Hynix HBM4 ramp delay

flags.headline: MRVL -5.69%: AI capex moderation + CFO insider selling at 91x P/E — escalated RED via thesis cross-check
flags.detail: Marvell Technology fell 5.69% on Friday June 26 ($281.87 → $265.84). MRVL had surged +32% on June 2 after Jensen Huang called it 'the next trillion-dollar company.' CFO insider selling of 211,000 shares at 91x trailing P/E adds asymmetric downside risk. AI capex moderation risk matches key_risk #3, triggering severity escalation. Custom silicon and 1.6T networking thesis remains intact.
flags.thesis_link: Matches MRVL key_risk #3: 'AI capex cycle moderation — if hyperscalers reduce data center investment due to ROI concerns or macro headwinds, Marvell's growth trajectory toward $15B revenue by FY2028 would be at risk.' Severity escalated AMBER → RED per thesis-match protocol.

evidence.source: CNBC — Jensen Huang calls MRVL 'next trillion-dollar company' June 2
Timothy Sykes — MRVL CFO sale and chip weakness

flags.headline: CAT -5.53%: Tariff headwind materializing but improving — estimate revised down, pre-market bounce +1.16%
flags.detail: Caterpillar fell 5.53% on Friday June 26 ($1,056.65 → $998.18). However: CAT is bouncing +1.16% pre-market Monday (~$1,009), and the White House announced reductions on certain industrial equipment tariffs, bringing CAT's own estimate DOWN from $2.6B to $2.2-2.4B for 2026. Q1 2026: 22% revenue surge, $63B backlog. The tariff risk is materializing but appears to be partially resolving. Keeping RED pending today's full session.
flags.thesis_link: Matches CAT key_risk #1: '$2.6B in incremental tariff costs in 2026 that could compress adjusted operating profit margins.' Tariff estimate has since been revised down to $2.2-2.4B — risk is materializing but partially resolving.

evidence.source: GuruFocus — CAT shares surge following tariff reductions

flags.headline: ADI -8.0%: Sector contagion; record Q2 revenue intact, no company-specific negative
flags.detail: Analog Devices fell 8.0% on Friday June 26, co-moving with the AI chip sector rout. No company-specific catalyst identified. Q2 FY2026 guidance $3.5B with ~47.5% adjusted operating margin. Data center revenue grew ~50% in FY2025. Thesis intact. HOLD name (score 78).

flags.headline: NXPI -7.65%: Sector contagion on a HOLD name; automotive recovery thesis intact
flags.detail: NXP Semiconductors fell 7.65% on Friday June 26 ($298.855 → $275.98), co-moving with the AI chip rout despite minimal direct AI capex exposure. Automotive recovery thesis intact: Q1 2026 revenue guided up 11% YoY. HOLD name (score 65).

flags.headline: Late-July hyperscaler earnings — capex commentary is highest-priority macro signal for 8 AI-infra holdings
flags.detail: Microsoft Q4 FY2026 earnings confirmed July 29. Amazon, Alphabet, and Meta Q2 2026 dates expected late July/early August. Together these four hyperscalers pledged $750B+ in collective 2026 capex. Any revision to data center spending guidance will directly move CRDO, MRVL, AMD, NVDA, LRCX, KEYS, TSM, and ADI. MSFT July 29 is the first read.

evidence.source: Microsoft Investor Relations — Q4 FY2026 earnings call July 29

deal_watch.terms: $24.55 take-private (Permira / Warburg Pincus / Francisco Partners / Temasek)
deal_watch.note: Confirmed closed June 25 at $24.55/share. CWAN delisted from NYSE. Remove from portfolios.json growth sleeve. Acquirers: Permira + Warburg Pincus + Francisco Partners + Temasek.

levels.note: SPY is 11.8% above the 645.80 retracement support set April 13, 2026. No breach risk. SPY closed Friday June 26 at $732.14 (-0.54%). QQQ closed at $704.94 (-1.49%).
